extraction.one_line_summary: CARTHY Hugh soloed to victory on Angliru after CARAPAZ Richard attacked to take red jersey from ROGLIC Primoz by 10 seconds.
tactical_patterns: CARAPAZ attacked precisely late enough to gain jersey but not destroy ROGLIC completely (10s gap), preserving team dynamics.
MOOS Enrique's long-range aggression created platform for CARTHY's stage win, showing domestique/leader separation tactics on Angliru.
WELLS Guillaume positioned in early breakaway suggests Lotto deliberately hedging GC riders across multiple groups on mountain stages.

rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: First GT stage win on Angliru with 16-second margin over chasers shows emergence as legitimate GC climber
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: Lost jersey defending rather than attacking, commentary suggests Tour TT weakness now systemic concern
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Calculated attack to take jersey without burning bridges, closed to CARTHY within 800m before fading tactically
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Finished second on stage, moved into top 10 GC, rode independently of team leader dynamics
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: Moved to 6th GC despite difficult conditions, commentary notes sustained march through standings
rider_signals.MOOS Enrique: positive: Long-range attack facilitated others' success while extending white jersey lead to near-insurmountable margin
